steps.title: Rewrite as a quadratic
steps.content: Expand the left side and move all terms to one side: $$x(kx-120)=-25$$ $$kx^2-120x+25=0$$

steps.title: Require two real solutions
steps.content: A quadratic has two distinct real solutions when its discriminant is positive. Here $a=k$, $b=-120$, and $c=25$, so the condition is $$(-120)^2-4(k)(25)>0$$

steps.title: Choose the greatest integer
steps.content: Solve the inequality to find the possible values of $k$: $$14{,}400-100k>0$$ $$144>k$$ The greatest integer less than $144$ is $143$. The answer is 143.
